_meta.provenance: PLAN-135 W5 unit o8o11o12 (O12 substrate watch) — the heroic S214/S230 manual changelog sweep + THIS plan's 8-dimension research sweep made a permanent nightly instrument. Consumed by .claude/scripts/check-substrate-watch.py; surfaced as nightly-hygiene dimension vii.
_meta._sources_note: claude_code URL swapped 2026-07-01: docs.claude.com/en/release-notes/claude-code now 301s to platform.claude.com which 307s to the GitHub CHANGELOG; fetch the raw GitHub head directly. PLAN-155 Wave 0 (debate A12): codex_cli + codex_harness source URLs added; a LIST value means the Owner --refresh fetches EACH URL for that key (the codex_harness entry watches the release feed AND the three host-harness doc pages — hook-schema drift can land in docs without a release-feed line).
_meta._source_stale_reason: Seeded last_seen from the values knowable at build time WITHOUT a network fetch (no-paid-spend rule; the changelog pages are not in-repo). The first Owner-run --refresh sets these to the live page heads and flips source_stale=false. Until then dimension vii reports a single advisory finding rather than a false 'up to date'.
_meta.refresh_recipe: Owner-run: WebFetch each source URL, read the topmost version + date, update the matching last_seen.{version,date} + set _meta.fetched + _meta.source_stale=false. Costs no model tokens (doc fetch only). Record nothing billable. This is the ONE network step O12 needs and it is PENDING-OWNER by design (agents stay no-network under ADR-136-AMEND-1).
_meta.notes: last_seen is the substrate version this framework was last RECONCILED against (its hooks/instruments/docs assume that surface). A newer upstream head is not a defect — it is a prompt to re-run the harvest/verify-the-knob-routes pass before trusting an old assumption (the S217/S228 silent-knob class). Detection only; never a BREAK.

components.label: Claude Code CLI
components.watch_for: settings.json schema changes (statusLine.refreshInterval — unverified per W5 context pack §3), new hook events, output-token env honoring (CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS S229), settingSources/--bare/strictMcpConfig flag drift (the O5 hermeticity surface).

components.label: Claude Agent SDK (TypeScript)
components.watch_for: agent-loop / tool-permission API changes that the framework's spawn + workflow primitives mirror.

components.label: Claude Agent SDK (Python)
components.watch_for: headless `claude -p` credit-bucket accounting (the 2026-06-15 Agent-SDK bucket axis), output-format / stream-json contract used by eval-baseline-n20 + w0b.

components.label: Codex CLI (pair-rail reviewer)
components.watch_for: codex `exec` flag-surface drift (the PLAN-142 / S214-S230 class): --color, -s/--sandbox enum (read-only|workspace-write|danger-full-access), -o/--output-last-message, --output-schema, --json (JSONL event stream), -m/--model; AND the removals that broke the rail on 0.139 — the old read-only/no-color/strict-json flags, and resume becoming an `exec resume` subcommand. All CLI shape lives in .claude/hooks/_lib/codex_cli_shape.py so a new removal/rename there is a NON-kernel edit. Also watch the binary-SHA pin (codex-cli-binary-sha256.txt) + the semver pin (codex-cli-pin.txt) when the binary upgrades.

components.label: Codex CLI (host harness: hooks/config/rules)
components.watch_for: PLAN-155 Codex-as-HOST schema surface (debate A12), verified against codex-cli 0.139.0 (S261/S262 research): lifecycle-hook envelope vocabulary (hookEventName / permissionDecision / permissionDecisionReason / hookSpecificOutput / additionalContext), PreToolUse interception coverage (official caveat: intercepts only 'the simple' shell calls today), apply_patch|Edit|Write matcher aliases, /hooks trust-hash keying semantics, .codex/hooks.json + [hooks]-in-config.toml registration shape, execpolicy prefix_rule syntax, requirements.toml MANAGED-hook semantics, project_doc_max_bytes (AGENTS.md 32 KiB cap), SubagentStart continue:false enforcement (parsed-but-NOT-enforced on 0.139 — a future release enforcing it promotes the spawn rail ADVISORY->ENFORCED), Stop decision:block semantics, ConfigChange lifecycle event appearance (ABSENT on 0.139). On drift the checker attaches the code-registered fixture re-record runbook (_DRIFT_RUNBOOKS in check-substrate-watch.py): bump the pin via the ADR-111 ceremony FIRST, then re-record the Wave-1 host-adapter fixtures.
